questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a box-and-whisker plot?

options.A: To display the mean of a dataset
options.B: To visualize the distribution of data
options.D: To identify the mode of a dataset
options.E: To determine the range of a dataset

questions.question: A shipping company has the following delivery times (in hours): $2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14$. What is the first quartile ($Q_1$)?
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a box-and-whisker plot?
A traffic study recorded the following travel times (in minutes): $15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50$. What is the interquartile range ($IQR$)?
Which of the following statements is TRUE about outliers in a box-and-whisker plot?

options.A: Outliers are always above the upper whisker
options.B: Outliers are always below the lower whisker
options.C: Outliers can be either above the upper whisker or below the lower whisker
options.D: Outliers are never shown in a box-and-whisker plot
options.E: Outliers are only shown if they are more than $2$ times the $IQR$ away from the median

questions.question: A logistics company has the following package weights (in pounds): $5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35$. What is the third quartile ($Q_3$)?
Which of the following types of data is most suitable for a box-and-whisker plot?
A transportation company recorded the following fuel efficiencies (in miles per gallon): $20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50$. What is the median of the data?

A shipping company has the following delivery times (in days): $3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15$. Construct a box-and-whisker plot to display the data and identify any outliers. Show all work and explain your reasoning.

Compare and contrast the use of box-and-whisker plots and histograms for displaying the distribution of quantitative data. Provide an example of a scenario where each type of plot would be more suitable. Show all work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of quartiles and interquartile range
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of identifying outliers in a dataset
Tip 3: Encourage students to use real-world examples to illustrate the application of box-and-whisker plots
